Title: Mechanisms of feedback inhibition and sequential firing of active sites in plant aspartate transcarbamoylase

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Transcarbamoylase family; catalytic homotrimer; chloroplast transit peptide; CP-loop and Asp-loop
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry/committed step of plastidial UMP biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major regulated enzyme; key control point for de novo UMP synthesis in plants
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses aspartate and carbamoyl phosphate to form carbamoyl aspartate in the first plastidial step of de novo pyrimidine synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for UMP and all pyrimidine nucleotides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of all pyrimidine nucleotides; required for nucleic acids and as cofactors in synthesis of sugars, polysaccharides, glycoproteins, and phospholipids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcription is regulated by tissue pyrimidine availability and TOR-mediated growth signals
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf-expressed; transcript and protein experimentally altered in Arabidopsis transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast; can be anchored to the inner plastid membrane
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ATC downregulation strongly inhibits Arabidopsis growth and photosynthetic efficiency, whereas overexpression enhances growth; enzyme activity is feedback-inhibited by UMP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Artificial microRNA knockdown; constitutive overexpression; qPCR; immunoblotting; growth and PAM fluorometry phenotyping; recombinant enzyme kinetics; isothermal titration calorimetry; X-ray crystallography; PALA inhibition; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day/standard growth conditions; exogenous PALA treatment; UMP, CP and Asp in biochemical assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S-CaMV constitutive promoter for amiRNA knockdown; ubiquitin-10 constitutive promoter for overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ATC downregulation causes severe growth reduction, chlorosis, and reduced photosynthetic efficiency; constitutive activation/UMP-insensitive variants are proposed but possible broader nucleotide-homeostasis tradeoffs were not tested
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to modulate pyrimidine synthesis and plant growth; overexpression may enhance biomass; engineered UMP-insensitive ATC variants could generate constitutively active enzymes for crop improvement; ATC also proposed as herbicide target
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Plant ATC is uniquely feedback-inhibited by UMP at the active site rather than a separate allosteric pocket. The enzyme functions as a homotrimer and structural data indicate sequential firing of active sites, with only one subunit attaining the closed catalytic conformation at a time
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis ATC is essential for efficient de novo pyrimidine synthesis-linked growth and photosynthesis. 2. UMP directly binds the ATC active site and competes with substrates, explaining plant-specific feedback inhibition. 3. ATC overexpression increases growth, while knockdown severely reduces biomass and photosynthetic performance. 4. Structural and biochemical analyses indicate communication among subunits and sequential firing of active sites.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Transcarbamoylase family; CP-loop mutant affecting conserved F161 residue
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry/committed step of plastidial UMP biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Alters regulatory control of the key ATC step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the same condensation reaction as wild-type ATC but with altered feedback regulation and active-site coordination
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for UMP while escaping UMP feedback inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: F161A abolishes UMP inhibition without major loss of catalytic efficiency, increases affinity for CP and PALA, and allows three PALA molecules to bind per trimer
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis; enzyme kinetics; ITC; X-ray crystallography
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UMP, PALA, CP and Asp in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shows substrate inhibition at high substrate concentrations, unlike wild type, implying loss of coordinated sequential catalysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides a template for engineering constitutively active, UMP-insensitive ATC variants to enhance crop growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: F161 in the CP-loop has a dual role: stabilizing the UMP-inhibited conformation and preventing simultaneous active conformation of all three subunits; its replacement with alanine removes steric constraints around the trimer axis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F161A is sufficient to render plant ATC insensitive to UMP. 2. The mutant retains catalytic activity near wild type but binds CP and PALA more strongly. 3. F161A permits binding of three PALA molecules per trimer, unlike WT. 4. Loss of F161 causes substrate inhibition, supporting a role for F161 in sequential firing and coordination of active sites.
